title: Radiant Saudi Deodeok Soup
description: A funky, glassy-jelly soup that blends Korean deodeok root with a monsoon-inspired Saudi flavor profile, featuring bhuna-spiced broth and ayurvedic Rajasic balance.
instructions: Heat a pot over medium heat and add the bhuna spice mix, stirring until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic, cooking until the onion becomes translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the sliced deodeok root, ground ginger, and ground cinnamon, sautéing for 2 minutes to coat the vegetables.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes until the root is tender.
Add the coconut milk and lemon juice, stirring to combine, then continue to simmer for an additional 5 minutes to meld flavors.
While the soup simmers, dissolve the agar-agar powder in 100ml of cold water, whisking until fully dissolved.
Add the agar-agar mixture to the soup pot, stirring constantly until the liquid thickens to a light gelatinous consistency, about 2 minutes.
Season with salt to taste, then remove the pot from heat and let the soup cool slightly to allow the gelatin to set.
Transfer the soup into serving bowls, allowing it to firm up into a glassy jelly over 5 minutes.
Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.
